gold.rationale: The game results and Final Four advancement are grounded in real reporting, but the article overstates how decisively Arizona separated from the field and reinforces that framing with an edited odds graphic presented as if it were straightforward market evidence. The sample therefore mixes true tournament facts with misleading context and manipulated visual support.

atomic_points.post_summary: The post reports that Arizona and Michigan advanced to the Final Four, with Arizona ending a 25-year drought by beating Purdue and becoming the betting favorite, while Michigan defeated Tennessee in a blowout.

sentence_level.sentence_text: Arizona and Michigan powered into the Final Four over the weekend, but by the time the bracket settled, the clearest focus was on Arizona.
atoms: Arizona advanced to the Final Four.
Michigan advanced to the Final Four.
The advancement occurred over the weekend.
The post claims the clearest focus was on Arizona.

sentence_level.sentence_text: The Wildcats’ run through the West and Michigan’s emphatic victory in the Midwest sent two in-form teams to Indianapolis, yet Arizona’s performance did the most to reshape the championship picture.

Arizona advanced through the West Region.
Michigan won in the Midwest Region.
The post describes Michigan's victory as emphatic.

The post claims Arizona's performance reshaped the championship picture.

atoms: Koa Peat scored 20 points.

atoms: Yaxel Lendeborg scored 27 points.
Elliot Cadeau had 10 assists.
Michigan is referred to as the Wolverines.
The post describes the game as a blowout.

sentence_level.sentence_text: Those results did more than complete half of the national semifinal field.
atoms: The results completed half of the national semifinal field.

sentence_level.sentence_text: They sharpened the race at the top of the tournament, and Arizona’s win in particular changed the tone around the title chase.
atoms: The post claims Arizona's win changed the tone around the title chase.

sentence_level.sentence_text: The Wildcats were already seen as a serious threat, but the way they handled Purdue — efficient on offense, strong on the glass and sharp defensively — created the feeling that a gap may now exist between Arizona and the rest of the field.
atoms: The post claims Arizona was seen as a serious threat.
The post claims Arizona was efficient on offense against Purdue.
The post claims Arizona was strong on the glass against Purdue.
The post claims Arizona was sharp defensively against Purdue.
The post claims a gap may exist between Arizona and the rest of the field.

sentence_level.sentence_text: Caption: Market snapshot entering Final Four week shows Arizona as the clear title front-runner after its Elite Eight statement.
atoms: The post claims Arizona is the clear title front-runner.
The post refers to Arizona's Elite Eight performance as a statement.

sentence_level.sentence_text: That market view quickly became part of the story.
atoms: The post claims the market view became part of the story.

sentence_level.sentence_text: Once the bracket locked in, the numbers swung sharply toward Arizona, and the Wildcats separated from the pack in a way that gave the Final Four a new center of gravity.
atoms: The post claims betting numbers swung sharply toward Arizona.
The post claims Arizona separated from the pack.

sentence_level.sentence_text: Whether through betting boards, prediction-market chatter or the broader conversation around the tournament, Arizona emerged as the clear favorite — the team seen as having the best mix of star power, pace control and late-March confidence.
atoms: The post claims Arizona emerged as the clear favorite.
The post claims Arizona has the best mix of star power, pace control, and late-March confidence.

sentence_level.sentence_text: Michigan remained firmly in the mix after dismantling Tennessee, but Arizona’s performance against Purdue gave the Wildcats the kind of lift that often turns a contender into the standard everyone else is measured against.
atoms: The post claims Michigan dismantled Tennessee.
The post claims Arizona's performance turned them into the standard.

sentence_level.sentence_text: For Arizona, the breakthrough carried extra weight.
atoms: The post claims the breakthrough carried extra weight for Arizona.

sentence_level.sentence_text: The program had not reached the Final Four since 2001, and that absence had hovered over nearly every deep-tournament conversation involving the Wildcats.
atoms: Arizona had not reached the Final Four since 2001.

sentence_level.sentence_text: That drought is over now, and it ended with a team that showed little sign of carrying that burden.
atoms: The post claims Arizona showed little sign of carrying the burden of the drought.

sentence_level.sentence_text: Peat’s 20 points led the box score, but the larger impression mattered too.
atoms: Koa Peat's 20 points led the box score.

sentence_level.sentence_text: Arizona looked poised, balanced and in control from the opening stretch through the closing minutes.
atoms: The post claims Arizona looked poised, balanced, and in control.

sentence_level.sentence_text: It did not merely survive a regional final.
atoms: The post claims Arizona did not merely survive the regional final.

sentence_level.sentence_text: It took hold of it.
atoms: The post claims Arizona took hold of the regional final.

sentence_level.sentence_text: Michigan’s path looked different, though it was no less striking.
atoms: The post claims Michigan's path was striking.

sentence_level.sentence_text: The Wolverines never needed late-game drama against Tennessee.
atoms: The post claims Michigan did not need late-game drama against Tennessee.

sentence_level.sentence_text: They produced one of the tournament’s most one-sided regional final wins instead.
atoms: The post claims Michigan's win was one of the most one-sided in the tournament.

sentence_level.sentence_text: Lendeborg handled the scoring load with 27 points, Cadeau directed everything with 10 assists, and Michigan’s offense repeatedly found openings against a Tennessee defense that entered the game known for toughness and discipline.
atoms: Yaxel Lendeborg scored 27 points.
Elliot Cadeau had 10 assists.
The post claims Tennessee's defense was known for toughness and discipline.

sentence_level.sentence_text: By the final minutes, the only real question was how much of that showing would carry over into Indianapolis.
atoms: The post poses a question about Michigan's performance carrying over to Indianapolis.

sentence_level.sentence_text: Even so, as clips spread and reactions piled up online, Arizona drew the strongest praise.
atoms: The post claims Arizona drew the strongest praise online.

sentence_level.sentence_text: Social media conversation around the Wildcats kept circling back to the same point: this is the most complete team left in the field.
atoms: The post claims social media called Arizona the most complete team left.

sentence_level.sentence_text: Fans highlighted Arizona’s ability to defend without losing offensive rhythm, Peat’s production in a high-pressure setting, and the way the Wildcats seemed to meet every Purdue push before it grew into a real threat.
atoms: The post claims fans highlighted Arizona's defense without losing offensive rhythm.
The post claims fans highlighted Peat's production.
The post claims fans highlighted Arizona meeting Purdue pushes.

sentence_level.sentence_text: Highlights from San Jose moved quickly, and the response was about more than numbers.
atoms: The post claims highlights from San Jose moved quickly.

sentence_level.sentence_text: Arizona did not just look good.
atoms: The post claims Arizona looked good.

sentence_level.sentence_text: It looked in command.
atoms: The post claims Arizona looked in command.

sentence_level.sentence_text: That does not erase Michigan’s case.
atoms: The post acknowledges Michigan's case.

sentence_level.sentence_text: The Wolverines were overwhelming against Tennessee and head to the national semifinals with momentum few teams can match.
atoms: The post claims Michigan was overwhelming against Tennessee.
The post claims Michigan has unmatched momentum.

sentence_level.sentence_text: Cadeau’s playmaking and Lendeborg’s scoring give Michigan clear tournament-winning strengths, and a 33-point Elite Eight win is impossible to ignore.
atoms: The post claims Michigan won the Elite Eight by 33 points.

sentence_level.sentence_text: Still, the conversation at the top has shifted.
atoms: The post claims the conversation at the top has shifted.

sentence_level.sentence_text: Arizona’s ceiling — and the way it showed that ceiling on Saturday night — has made the Wildcats the reference point for the rest of the field.
atoms: The post claims Arizona is the reference point for the rest of the field.

sentence_level.sentence_text: Now the semifinal picture moves to Indianapolis, where Arizona and Michigan are set to meet on one side of the bracket, with UConn and Illinois on the other.
atoms: The semifinals are in Indianapolis.
Arizona and Michigan are on one side of the bracket.
UConn and Illinois are on the other side of the bracket.

sentence_level.sentence_text: That gives the Final Four an immediate heavyweight feel, with two teams arriving off dominant regional final performances and the winner of Arizona-Michigan likely carrying a fresh surge of championship momentum into the title game.
atoms: The post claims the Final Four has a heavyweight feel.
The post claims Arizona and Michigan had dominant regional final performances.

sentence_level.sentence_text: But even in a field this strong, Arizona enters the week with the clearest expectations.
atoms: The post claims Arizona enters with the clearest expectations.

sentence_level.sentence_text: The Wildcats ended a 25-year wait, delivered the defining performance of the weekend and now look like a team that has moved from contender to favorite.
atoms: Arizona ended a 25-year wait.
The post claims Arizona delivered the defining performance of the weekend.
The post claims Arizona moved from contender to favorite.

retrieved_evidence.text: Michigan’s offensive display in the Midwest Region final on March 29, 2026, was highlighted by 27 points from Yaxel Lendeborg and 10 assists from guard Elliot Cadeau, according to official box scores. Team representatives said Lendeborg’s scoring, combined with Cadeau’s distribution, helped establish a consistent rhythm that Tennessee struggled to disrupt. Analysts following the game noted that the pairing has emerged as a key axis of Michigan’s attack in recent weeks, although coaches cautioned that future opponents may adjust their defensive schemes accordingly.

SAN JOSE, Calif. — Arizona punched its ticket to the Final Four on March 28, 2026, beating Purdue 79-64 in the West Region final. Freshman Koa Peat scored 20 as the Wildcats ended a 25-year Final Four drought, AP reports.

Arizona advanced to the national semifinals after defeating Purdue 79-64 in the West Region final played on March 28, 2026, according to tournament officials. The game at a neutral-site arena saw Arizona establish a double-digit lead in the second half and maintain control despite several attempted comebacks by Purdue. Coaches on both sides acknowledged that the result reflected Arizona’s defensive intensity and balanced scoring, while emphasizing that final statistics and postgame evaluations would continue to shape assessments of both programs’ seasons.
CHICAGO — Michigan rolled into the Final Four on March 29, 2026, hammering Tennessee 95-62 in the Midwest Region final. Yaxel Lendeborg scored 27 and Elliot Cadeau had 10 assists, according to AP.

Michigan booked a place in the national semifinals by defeating Tennessee 95-62 in the Midwest Region final on March 29, 2026, according to tournament organizers. The matchup, held at a major indoor venue, saw Michigan pull away early behind efficient shooting and sustained defensive pressure, leading to one of the larger winning margins of the regional round. Tennessee’s coaching staff acknowledged the difficulty of overcoming such a deficit, while Michigan officials pointed to the result as confirmation of the team’s progression over the course of the season.

Betting analysts and college basketball commentators have highlighted the 2026 NCAA tournament by referencing prediction‑market style charts that assign changing probabilities to each contender, with Arizona shown as a clear national title favorite and Michigan trailing alongside other programs. In recent discussions, these graphics are described as cleanly branded, market‑like dashboards in which Arizona’s line surges well ahead of the field, reflecting how investors and fans are treating the Wildcats’ Final Four trip as a decisive shift in the championship outlook.

Arizona forward Koa Peat contributed a team-high 20 points in the West Region final on March 28, 2026, playing a central role in the program’s progression, according to game reports. Coaching staff noted that Peat’s scoring came at key moments in both halves, including several baskets that halted Purdue’s runs and helped stabilize Arizona’s offense. Observers at the arena added that his performance, which also included rebounds and defensive sequences, underscored his importance in the current rotation, though full postseason evaluations remain ongoing.

Arizona’s victory in the West Region final at the end of March 2026 ended a 25-year wait for a return to the Final Four, according to data compiled by college basketball historians. Local supporters and former players described the breakthrough as a significant milestone for a program that had repeatedly reached later tournament stages without advancing this far. University officials indicated that celebratory events would be held on campus in the coming days, while stressing that preparation for the upcoming national semifinal remains the team’s primary focus.

Michigan’s path to the 2026 Final Four has likewise been framed around on‑court celebrations, particularly moments in which two Wolverines players rejoice together after key baskets or defensive stands. In recent reports on the Elite Eight win over Tennessee, observers describe how the team’s energy, bench reactions and close‑knit chemistry underlined its resurgence on the national stage, positioning Michigan as a serious, if less heavily favored, challenger to Arizona in the later rounds of the NCAA tournament.

Bryce James has more Final Four appearances than Texas A&M has all time.

NO ONE has looked more impressive than Arizona this year.

Arizona’s recent performances in the national tournament have drawn sustained praise across social media platforms, according to monitoring of public commentary in late March 2026. Posts from basketball commentators, former players and general fans have highlighted the team’s defensive improvements, depth of scoring options and apparent composure in high-pressure situations. While such reactions are subjective and vary by source, communications staff at the university acknowledged that the surge in online attention has contributed to heightened visibility for the program during its current postseason run.

Arizona’s run to the 2026 Final Four has been described in recent reports through exuberant moments of celebration, with key players lifting both arms toward the rafters as a wave of fans reacts in packed arenas during the NCAA tournament. According to commentators who have followed the program’s history of earlier disappointments, this breakthrough is portrayed as a turning point that has altered expectations around the national championship race, reinforcing Arizona’s status as a dominant, emotionally charged presence in March Madness.

The 2026 NCAA tournament coverage frequently situates Arizona and Michigan within the broader spectacle of the Final Four, referencing large arenas dressed in official March Madness branding and prominently displayed national brackets tracking each round. According to sports reporters, the national semifinal field crystallized over a dramatic Elite Eight weekend, with the bracket boards and court signage helping fans follow the progression from regional finals to the final quartet of teams vying for the championship in an event that has become a staple of American sports culture.

As the national title race enters its closing stages, analysts say prediction markets and sports betting discussions are increasingly being treated as part of the broader framing of championship prospects. Market movements and odds updates in recent days have been cited by some commentators as informal indicators of perceived momentum among leading teams, although they do not constitute official forecasts. Regulatory authorities and league officials have reiterated that such activities are governed by existing rules, and that on-court results remain the sole determinant of tournament outcomes.

text_analysis.alignment_reasoning: The post accurately reports the core game results and player stats, but presents specific betting odds (48% for Arizona) that contradict the visual evidence provided in the post's own graphic (58% for Arizona), and uses slightly inflated language ('blowout', 'clear favorite') compared to the neutral evidence.
text_analysis.t2_evidence: The post describes Michigan's win as a 'blowout' and claims Arizona is the 'clear favorite' with 'clearest expectations', whereas the evidence (news_458_5, news_458_7) uses more measured terms like 'hammering', 'consistent rhythm', and 'significant milestone' without declaring a definitive favorite or using the term 'blowout'.
t2_problematic_subclaims: Michigan rolled past Tennessee... turned a regional final into a blowout
Arizona emerged as the clear favorite
Arizona enters the week with the clearest expectations
text_analysis.c1_evidence: The post text explicitly lists Arizona's championship odds as '48%' in the graphic section, but the provided image description for image_2 (02_img_2.jpg) shows OCR text 'ARIZ 58%', creating a direct numerical contradiction between the text claim and the visual evidence.

images.scene_description: A basketball player wearing a dark blue jersey with 'UCONN' and the number '2' is shown from the chest up, raising both hands in a defensive or rebounding stance. The background shows a crowded arena with spectators.
images.v2_evidence: The image depicts a UConn player, but the post text and evidence describe the image as visual evidence of the Arizona team celebrating their advancement.
images.c_evidence: The image shows a UConn player, while the text and evidence claim it depicts the Arizona team.
images.explanation: The image is a genuine photo of a UConn player, but it is misused to represent the Arizona team mentioned in the text.

images.scene_description: A line chart titled 'Men's College Basketball Champion' from 'Kalshi' showing betting odds. The red line for 'ARIZ' is at 58%, the dark blue line for 'MICH' is at 24%, and another blue line for 'ILL' is at 11%.
images.ocr_text: BASKETBALL | Men's College Basketball Champion | ARIZ 58% | MICH 24% | ILL 11% | Kalshi | + $600 | + $33 | + $9 | + $100
images.c_evidence: The chart shows odds of 58% for Arizona and 24% for Michigan, whereas the post text explicitly lists the odds as 48% for Arizona and 27% for Michigan.
images.explanation: The chart is a real graphic but contains specific data values (58%, 24%) that contradict the specific values cited in the post text (48%, 27%).

images.scene_description: Two basketball players in white Michigan jerseys are shaking hands and celebrating. The player on the right has the number 23 and the name 'MICHIGAN' on his jersey. The player on the left has the number 1 and the name 'MCKENNEY' on his back.
images.v2_evidence: The image shows players McKenney (#1) and Lendeborg (#23), but the post text claims the image illustrates players Lendeborg and Cadeau.
images.c_evidence: The image features player McKenney, while the text claims the image shows Cadeau.
images.explanation: The image is a genuine photo of Michigan players, but it is misidentified in the text as showing Cadeau instead of McKenney.

images.scene_description: A wide shot of a basketball court with a large '2026 NCAA MEN'S FINAL FOUR INDIANAPOLIS' logo painted at center court. The arena seats are mostly empty, and staff members are visible on the sidelines.
images.explanation: This is a clean, accurate image of the venue described in the text.

image_analysis.global_notes: The post contains multiple mismatches between the text claims and the visual evidence: Image 1 shows UConn instead of Arizona; Image 2 shows different betting odds than cited in the text; Image 3 shows McKenney instead of Cadeau.

judge.level3_rationale: Although the post correctly identifies the teams advancing to the Final Four and their game scores, it presents misleading visual evidence by using a chart with incorrect odds percentages and a court logo from the wrong year (2025 instead of 2026).
judge.reasoning_trace: The post's core narrative is supported by news evidence. However, the text contradicts the provided chart image regarding betting odds (48% vs 58%), triggering C1. The first image is of a UConn player, which is contextually inconsistent with the text's primary focus on Arizona and Michigan, triggering C2. Two L2 labels, no T1/V1, leads to Mostly True.
judge.core_claim: Arizona and Michigan advanced to the 2026 Final Four in Indianapolis, with Arizona becoming the clear betting favorite after ending a 25-year drought.
key_subclaims: Arizona beat Purdue 79-64 in the West Region final on March 28 in San Jose.
Koa Peat scored 20 points for Arizona.
Michigan beat Tennessee 95-62 in the Midwest Region final in Chicago.
Yaxel Lendeborg scored 27 points and Elliot Cadeau had 10 assists for Michigan.
The provided chart shows Arizona's odds at 58%.
The provided images depict the specific players and teams mentioned in the text (Arizona, Michigan, UConn).
The Final Four is taking place in Indianapolis in 2026.

subclaim_findings.subclaim: Arizona beat Purdue 79-64 in the West Region final on March 28 in San Jose.
subclaim_findings.note: Evidence confirms the score, date, and location.

subclaim_findings.subclaim: Koa Peat scored 20 points for Arizona.
Michigan beat Tennessee 95-62 in the Midwest Region final in Chicago.
subclaim_findings.note: Evidence confirms the score, date, and location.

subclaim_findings.subclaim: Yaxel Lendeborg scored 27 points and Elliot Cadeau had 10 assists for Michigan.

subclaim_findings.note: The text claims 48%, but the provided chart image explicitly shows 58%.

subclaim_findings.subclaim: The provided chart shows Arizona's odds at 58%.
subclaim_findings.note: Visual inspection of the chart confirms ARIZ 58%.

subclaim_findings.subclaim: The provided images depict the specific players and teams mentioned in the text (Arizona, Michigan, UConn).
subclaim_findings.note: Image 1 shows a UConn player, but the text focuses on Arizona and Michigan. The text does not describe a UConn player, making the image contextually mismatched or misleadingly paired.

subclaim_findings.subclaim: The Final Four is taking place in Indianapolis in 2026.
subclaim_findings.note: Image 4 shows the 2026 Final Four Indianapolis logo.

judge.verdict_reasoning: The core claim that Arizona and Michigan advanced to the Final Four is true and supported by evidence. However, there are two distinct inconsistencies: 1) The text states Arizona's odds are 48%, while the provided chart image shows 58% (C1 Semantic Inconsistency). 2) The first image shows a UConn player, which is contextually mismatched with the text's focus on Arizona and Michigan (C2 Contextual Inconsistency). These are factual errors/inconsistencies but do not negate the main event reporting. With exactly 2 L2 labels and no fabrication (T1) or synthetic visuals (V1), the verdict is Mostly True.

image_findings.explanation: The image shows a UConn player, but the text narrative focuses on Arizona and Michigan, creating a semantic and contextual mismatch as UConn is only a minor mention.
The text claims Arizona's odds are 48%, but the image clearly displays 58%, creating a direct semantic contradiction.
The image shows Michigan players, consistent with the text's discussion of Michigan.
The image shows the Final Four logo in Indianapolis, consistent with the text.
